title: Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building
summary: A fast way to select the lowest small interior space away from glass and wide roofs when destructive rotating winds threaten an unfamiliar building.
content:
A fast way to select the lowest small interior space away from glass and wide roofs when destructive rotating winds threaten an unfamiliar building.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Ask staff or inspect signs immediately for a basement, designated shelter, interior stairwell, or small windowless room on the lowest safe level.
Avoid halls beneath wide-span roofs, gymnasiums, warehouses, exterior rooms, atriums, and lifts.
Crouch low, cover head and neck with arms and sturdy padding, keep shoes on, and assist mobility-impaired people without delaying shelter.
Remain sheltered until official information indicates the threat has passed, then check for fire, gas, wiring, and structural damage before moving.
warnings: A vehicle, mobile home, or room with many windows is poor shelter from destructive wind.
Do not leave shelter merely because the wind becomes briefly quiet.
common_mistakes: Starting Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Rotating-wind shelter in an unfamiliar building.']
